core.label: TypeScript Fundamentals
core.description: Setup, basic types, and the type system foundation.

topics.label: Installation & Configuration
topics.description: Install TypeScript, configure tsconfig.json, and understand compiler options. tsconfig controls how strict TS is.

topics.label: Basic Types
topics.description: string, number, boolean, null, undefined, any, unknown, never, void. The foundation of TypeScript's type system.

topics.label: Type Annotations & Inference
topics.description: Explicitly annotate types or let TypeScript infer them. TS infers most types — only annotate when needed.

topics.label: Arrays & Tuples
topics.description: Typed arrays (string[]) and fixed-length tuples ([string, number]). Tuples are useful for return types.

topics.label: Object Types
topics.description: Inline object types, optional properties (?), and readonly. Object types describe the shape of data.

topics.label: Union & Intersection Types
topics.description: Combine types with | (union) and & (intersection). Unions model values that can be one of several types.

topics.label: Type Aliases & Interfaces
topics.description: Name types with type and interface. Interfaces are extendable; type aliases are more flexible.

topics.label: Literal Types
topics.description: Narrow types to specific values — 'hello', 42, true. Literal types enable precise type checking.

topics.label: Enums
topics.description: Numeric and string enums for named constants. Prefer const enums or union types for tree-shaking.

topics.label: Type Assertions
topics.description: Override inferred types with as. Use sparingly — assertions bypass type checking.

core.label: Functions & Generics
core.description: Typed functions, overloads, and generic programming.

topics.label: Function Types
topics.description: Type parameters and return types. Function type expressions describe callback shapes.

topics.label: Optional & Default Parameters
topics.description: Optional (param?) and default (param = value) parameters in typed functions.

topics.label: Rest Parameters & Spread
topics.description: Typed rest parameters (...args: string[]) and spread in function calls.

topics.label: Function Overloads
topics.description: Multiple function signatures for different input/output combinations. Overloads describe complex APIs.

topics.label: Generics Basics
topics.description: Write reusable functions and types with type parameters <T>. Generics are TypeScript's most powerful feature.

topics.label: Generic Constraints
topics.description: Constrain type parameters with extends. Constraints ensure the generic type has required properties.

topics.label: Generic Utility Types
topics.description: Partial, Required, Pick, Omit, Record, Readonly, ReturnType, Parameters. Built-in type transformations.

topics.label: typeof & keyof
topics.description: Extract types from values (typeof) and keys (keyof). Powerful for deriving types from existing code.

core.label: Advanced Types
core.description: Narrowing, discriminated unions, and type manipulation.

topics.label: Type Narrowing
topics.description: Narrow types with typeof, instanceof, in, and truthiness checks. Narrowing is how TS understands control flow.

topics.label: Discriminated Unions
topics.description: Union types with a common literal discriminant field. The most important pattern for modeling state in TS.

topics.label: Type Guards
topics.description: User-defined type guards with is return type. Custom narrowing for complex type checks.

topics.label: Mapped Types
topics.description: Transform properties of existing types with [K in keyof T]. Mapped types create new types from old ones.

topics.label: Conditional Types
topics.description: T extends U ? X : Y for type-level branching. Powers complex utility types and library APIs.

topics.label: Template Literal Types
topics.description: String manipulation at the type level. Create typed string patterns like `${string}/${string}`.

topics.label: Indexed Access Types
topics.description: Access nested types with Type['property']. Extract types from arrays, objects, and tuples.

topics.label: infer Keyword
topics.description: Extract types within conditional types with infer. Powers ReturnType, Parameters, and custom extractions.

core.label: Classes & OOP
core.description: Typed classes, access modifiers, and decorators.

topics.label: Class Basics
topics.description: Typed fields, constructors, methods, and parameter properties. TS classes add type safety to JS classes.

topics.label: Access Modifiers
topics.description: public, private, protected, and #private fields. Control member visibility at compile time.

topics.label: Abstract Classes
topics.description: Define partial implementations that must be extended. Abstract classes are templates for derived classes.

topics.label: Implementing Interfaces
topics.description: Classes implement interfaces with implements keyword. Enforces contracts on class structure.

topics.label: Generic Classes
topics.description: Parameterize classes with type parameters. Generic classes create reusable, type-safe containers.

topics.label: Decorators
topics.description: Annotate classes and members with decorators (Stage 3). Used heavily in Angular, NestJS, and TypeORM.

topics.label: Mixins
topics.description: Compose class behavior from multiple sources. TypeScript's pattern for multiple inheritance.

topics.label: this Type
topics.description: Use this as a return type for fluent APIs and builder patterns. Enables method chaining with proper types.

core.label: Modules & Project Setup
core.description: Module systems, declaration files, and project configuration.

topics.label: ES Module Syntax
topics.description: import/export with type annotations. TypeScript extends JS modules with type-only imports.

topics.label: Type-Only Imports
topics.description: import type { T } to import only type information. Ensures types are erased at compile time.

topics.label: Declaration Files (.d.ts)
topics.description: Describe types for JS libraries. DefinitelyTyped (@types/*) provides types for popular packages.

topics.label: tsconfig Deep Dive
topics.description: strict, target, module, moduleResolution, paths, and baseUrl. Config controls TS's behavior and strictness.

topics.label: Project References
topics.description: Split large projects into sub-projects with composite and references. Enables incremental builds.

topics.label: Path Aliases
topics.description: Configure import aliases with paths in tsconfig. Clean imports like @/components instead of ../../../.

topics.label: Ambient Declarations
topics.description: Declare types for global variables and modules without implementations. Used for browser globals and legacy code.

topics.label: Monorepo Setup
topics.description: Configure TS in monorepos with project references and tools like Turborepo or Nx.

core.label: Patterns & Best Practices
core.description: Idiomatic TypeScript patterns, strictness, and code quality.

topics.label: Strict Mode
topics.description: Enable strict: true for maximum type safety. Strict mode catches more bugs and is recommended for all projects.

topics.label: Branded Types
topics.description: Create nominal types with branding for type-safe IDs and units. Prevents mixing incompatible values.

topics.label: Exhaustiveness Checking
topics.description: Use never in switch defaults to ensure all cases are handled. Catch missing cases at compile time.

topics.label: Type vs Interface Guidelines
topics.description: When to use type vs interface. Interfaces for objects and extension; types for unions and complex types.

topics.label: Zod & Runtime Validation
topics.description: Validate data at runtime with Zod and infer TypeScript types from schemas. Bridges runtime and compile-time safety.

topics.label: ESLint for TypeScript
topics.description: Configure typescript-eslint for TS-aware linting rules. Catches type-related bugs ESLint alone misses.

topics.label: Testing TypeScript
topics.description: Test TS with Vitest or Jest with ts-jest. Type testing with expect-type for library authors.

topics.label: Migration from JavaScript
topics.description: Incrementally adopt TypeScript in existing JS projects with allowJs and checkJs.
